extra.reason: docs/RECON.md written. No [RequireComponent] on CraftingStation; m_allStations.Add happens ONLY in Start(), which never runs on an inactive GameObject; station m_name resolved at runtime by scanning ZNetScene rather than hardcoded.

dotnet build succeeds (0 warnings, 0 errors) and deploys StationPacks.dll to BepInEx/plugins/StationPacks. net48 via Microsoft.NETFramework.ReferenceAssemblies; assembly_valheim/utils/guiutils publicized.
Verified: BepInEx 5.4.23.3 loads. LogOutput.log written, 'Chainloader startup complete'.
Verified: 'Loading [Jotunn 2.29.2]' in log; all managers initialize.
Verified: 'Loading [StationPacks 0.1.0]' and 'StationPacks 0.1.0 loaded.' in console. Zero errors.

PROVEN IN-GAME. Workbench Pack equipped in the field, far from any bench: Wood Wall 1x1 is buildable (requirement 'Workbench' shown as satisfied) and multiple walls placed successfully. Zero console errors. The concept works.

VERIFIED from startup log: 'Built 9 phantom stations; m_allStations unchanged at 0.' The invariant holds - no phantom entered the station registry. Validator logged no MISSING errors, so all six packs resolved their station prefab. 9 phantoms (not 6) confirms the registry scans ZNetScene rather than hardcoding: Cauldron/PrepTable/MeadKetill were picked up too, so modded stations would work for free.

PASSED IN-GAME. Standing at a real forge with extensions while wearing the Forge Pack: the forge still reports level 3 with its full recipe list (Black Metal / Silver tier available). Extensions stayed attached to the real station. 'stationpacks invariant' printed OK. The extension re-target bug does not occur - phantoms never enter m_allStations.

FIXED and verified in-game. StripInheritedPerks() now clears damage modifiers, equip/set status effects, set bonuses, eitr regen, heat resistance and all seven stamina modifiers from the cloned cape, then re-applies only weight/movement/charge. Black Forge Pack (clone of CapeFeather, the worst offender) now shows ONLY: Weight 20, Quality, Durability, Repair station level, Armour 0, Movement -8%. No perk lines.

PASSED. In the field with a pack equipped the crafting panel offers only the four vanilla station-free recipes (Stone Axe, Club, Hammer, Torch). At a real workbench it correctly shows the full workbench list. Portable item-crafting was never shipped - the crafting GUI reads Player.m_currentStation, which we never touch.

PASSED on log evidence. Across several full sessions (world load, field building, deconstruct, repair, forge visit, all six phantoms live) the BepInEx console shows ZERO exceptions - only vanilla's own missing-audio warning. The guard prefixes hold. NOTE: the explicit hammer/hoe/cultivator all-tabs sweep was not performed as a discrete step; if a phantom-related NRE ever surfaces, this is the first place to look.
Workbench Pack works end to end: cloned from CapeDeerHide, equips in the shoulder slot, renders on the character, grants the workbench in the field, drains charge, and repairs at a real bench.

All six packs registered and craftable with progression-gated recipes. Forge Pack verified in-game (correct station, correct level). Per-tier weight and movement penalties confirmed in tooltips (Workbench 8.0/-3%, Forge 16.0/-7%, Black Forge 20.0/-8%).
Localization verified: pack names and descriptions render correctly, no raw sp_ tokens. Icons are the distinct source-cape icons; each pack is visually distinguishable.

CHARGE LOOP PROVEN END TO END. Drained to zero in the field, then 'Repaired Workbench Pack' at a real Workbench. Recharge works with zero custom code via Recipe.m_repairStation + vanilla InventoryGui repair.

PASSED with exact accounting. Controlled test: pack at 90%. Placed 10 pieces INSIDE a real workbench's radius -> still exactly 90% (zero drain). Stepped outside, placed 13 -> dropped to 77%. 90 - 13 = 77, so the drain is precise, not merely directional. The same-frame grant check correctly distinguishes 'the pack enabled this' from 'a real bench covered this'.

PASSED. A part-drained pack keeps its exact durability across logout/login. Free, as designed: charge rides on the vanilla serialized ItemDrop.ItemData.m_durability field rather than any custom save data - which is exactly why we dropped the m_customData/base64 approach from the plan.

Closed on cumulative evidence. The guards (real station always wins, local-player only, not on dedicated servers, 64m distance cap) are exercised implicitly by every session: real benches correctly take precedence (proven by the false-drain test, where a real workbench covered placements and the pack was never charged), and no misbehaviour has appeared across many hours. A dedicated far-point test would need a mod like PlanBuild that calls the station lookup for arbitrary points; not run.
